vix_note: VIX closed 18.65 Monday; fell ~7% to ~17.33 in early Tuesday trading as ceasefire diplomacy eased oil prices
valuation_note: Forward P/E 20.3x is above both the 5-yr avg (19.9x) and 10-yr avg (19.0x); trailing P/E ~25.4x vs historical median 18.0x
futures_note: S&P 500 futures +0.45-0.49% early Tuesday; Nasdaq leading; chip stocks and Asian markets providing tailwind
fomc_note: Fed in blackout period now; July 29 decision at 2:00 PM ET; ~92% hold, ~7% 25bps hike; no SEP this meeting
cpi_latest: 4.2% YoY (May 2026), driven by 23.5% energy surge from Middle East tensions
oil_price_note: Crude near $90/barrel after Iran tensions; pulling back Tuesday on ceasefire diplomacy
geopolitical: Iran-US tensions ongoing; ceasefire broke down mid-July; 10 consecutive nights of US strikes; mediators pushing for 10-day ceasefire as of July 21
tariff_note: Trump imposed 50% tariffs on Canadian goods (dairy, chemicals) effective in 30 days; Canadian oil exempt
events: Tue Jul 21 pre-mkt: GM, HAL, MMM, NVS, SCHW earnings
Wed Jul 22: GOOGL, IBM earnings (AI spending in focus)
Thu Jul 23: INTC, RTX earnings; TSLA also this week
Tue Jul 29 2:00 PM ET: FOMC rate decision (hold expected, ~7% hike risk)
No CPI this week (next: Aug 12); no jobs report this week (next: Aug 7)
note: Cautious: market is in structural uptrend and VIX is moderate, but forward P/E (20.3x) is above historical averages, CPI inflation is running at 4.2% YoY (well above the 2% Fed target), FOMC meets in 8 days with non-trivial hike risk, Iran geopolitics unresolved, and a dense earnings week (GOOGL, TSLA, IBM) creates near-term volatility potential. No thesis candidate clears the required margin of safety at current prices. Demand a bigger discount before any new buy.
